subject: Re: Seattle trip in December
body: Couch is yours, don't waste money on an Airbnb. I'm taking Fri off when Mia visits the weekend before so I'll be around. Let's do Rattlesnake Ledge if it's clear, otherwise we drink coffee and stare at the rack. Send me your flight times.\n\n— Jason

subject: Jake's birthday dinner — Oct 17
body: Booked the new sushi spot on Capitol Hill for the 17th, 7pm, table for 6. Reply if you're in so I can confirm the headcount. Jake doesn't know which place yet so keep it quiet.\n\n— Jason

subject: Re: glanceboard IPv6 false positive
body: Thanks for the report. I think the prober is defaulting to A records only — I'll add an AAAA fallback and a per-check address family flag. Pushing a fix to the branch tonight, can you test against your IPv6-only host after?\n\n— Jason

subject: Re: Thanksgiving flights
body: Found a nonstop SEA to SMF Wednesday night, lands around 9. I can do the red-eye back Sunday so I don't burn a vacation day. Don't pick me up that late, I'll grab the light rail and drive over in the morning. Tell Mia I'm bringing the good coffee.\n\n— Jason

subject: Re: Postgres connection pool tuning
body: I traced the timeouts to pgbouncer being in session mode while the workers assume transaction mode. Switching to transaction pooling and dropping max_client_conn to 200 cleared it in staging. Writing it up in the runbook before I roll to prod tomorrow.\n\n— Jason

subject: Re: Actions runner stuck on go-worker-pool
body: The hosted runner hangs on the race-detector job for go-worker-pool, but only on ubuntu-latest after the image bump. It passes on the pinned 22.04 image. Logs and a minimal repro link are below. Can you confirm whether the new image changed the default GOMAXPROCS in the container?\n\n— Jason Murray

subject: Re: raft-kv review
body: Thanks for going through it. You're right that the leader can apply before the commit index advances on a slow follower — I'll gate the apply loop on commitIndex and add a test that kills a follower mid-append. Want to pair on the snapshotting bit Thursday after standup?\n\n— Jason

subject: Re: Ridgeline import from Pocket failing
body: Thanks for the bug report. The Pocket export uses a slightly different HTML format since their last update, so the parser drops items without a time_added attribute. I'm shipping a fix tonight that falls back to the file mtime — sorry about the lost tags, those should come through after you re-import.\n\n— Jason

subject: Re: internship resume
body: Looks solid. Two things: put the Go project at the top since that's what they'll care about, and quantify the data pipeline line — how many records, how much faster. Don't list every class. Send me the final and I'll forward it to Sofia, she's hiring interns this cycle.\n\n— Jason

subject: Re: Annual physical reschedule
body: Can we move my appointment with Dr. Kim from the 22nd to any morning the following week? Something came up at work that afternoon. Tuesday or Wednesday before 10am would be ideal. Thanks.\n\nJason Murray, DOB 02/14/2003

subject: Re: homelab GPU node
body: I'd skip the used 3090 for now — the homelab cluster is CPU-bound and the PSU in that node tops out at 550W. If you really want to mess with local models, throw it in your tower and we'll tailscale into it. Cheaper than re-racking mine.\n\n— Jason

subject: Re: go-rate-limiter PR
body: Merged it, thanks. The sliding-window log was using a slice you grew unbounded under burst — swapped it for a ring buffer and it's allocation-free now. Can you add a benchmark for the 10k-rps case so we catch regressions? I'll cut a tag once that lands.\n\n— Jason

subject: Submission: building a bounded worker pool in Go
body: Hi — I wrote up the design behind go-worker-pool (backpressure, graceful shutdown, generics) and thought it might fit a Spotlight slot. Post is at jason.murray.dev/posts/bounded-worker-pool. No worries if it's not a fit. Thanks for the newsletter, it's the first thing I read Friday mornings.\n\n— Jason Murray

subject: Re: on-call handoff Friday
body: I'll take the pager Friday so you can make your flight. Heads up: the ingest service has a flaky alert on p99 latency that's almost always GC during the nightly batch — check the dashboard before paging anyone. Runbook link is in the channel topic.\n\n— Jason

subject: 1:1 topics for Monday
body: Few things for our 1:1: I want to own the migration tooling work end to end, the Terraform module needs a review before I apply it to the prod account, and I'd like to talk about the senior track and what I should be aiming at this quarter. Also taking PTO the week of Thanksgiving.\n\n— Jason

subject: Board game night Saturday?
body: Thinking Saturday at my place, 7ish. I'll cook, someone bring the heavy euro game this time instead of another party deck. Capitol Hill, you know the address. Reply by Thursday so I know how much to make.\n\n— Jason

subject: Re: Personal account cost anomaly
body: I got the anomaly alert on my personal account — the spike is the EKS control plane I left running for terraform-aws-eks testing. That was intentional this month, please don't flag it. I'm tearing it down this weekend so next month should drop back to the usual S3 backup spend.\n\nJason Murray

subject: Re: Dad's birthday gift
body: I'm in for the woodworking class gift card — put me down for half. If you want to do the dinner too I can Venmo you for my share. I can't fly down that weekend but I'll call him and set up a screen-share so Mia can show him the photos.\n\n— Jason

subject: Re: glanceboard-agent memory leak
body: Found it — the agent kept a goroutine per check that never exited when a host went unreachable, so they piled up. Switched to a single ticker with context cancellation. Tagged v0.4.1, can you redeploy on your dozen-host setup and watch RSS overnight?\n\n— Jason

subject: Re: RIDGE-1482 migration rollback plan
body: Adding the rollback notes to the ticket: pg-migrate writes a down migration for 0042, but the column drop isn't reversible once data lands, so the rollback restores from the pre-migration snapshot instead. Snapshot is automated in the deploy job. Moving this to In Review.\n\n— Jason
